---
title: "RP 18 Part 2: Releasing Emotional Pressure Safely"
format: html
---
# RP 18 Part 2: Releasing Emotional Pressure Safely

After identifying emotional triggers, the next skill is emotional release without self-harm or drinking. Emotional release can include writing, movement, breathing, talking to safe people, self-compassion, creative expression, or structured problem-solving.

Avoidance usually increases emotional pressure; expression with structure usually reduces it.

Skillful emotional release does not erase discomfort instantly, but it often lowers intensity enough to restore choice. Repeated practice builds confidence that difficult emotions can be tolerated without drinking.

## Guided Release Practice
1. Name what you are feeling.
2. Rate intensity (0-10).
3. Choose one release method (journal, walk, breathing, call, grounding).
4. Re-rate intensity after 15-20 minutes.

::: {.callout-tip}
### Try this this week

Use the steps below as a practical experiment and notice what shifts.

Use this process four times this week and note which method lowers intensity fastest.

Briefly record what worked best so you can keep the strongest strategy.
:::
